summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that backtick-delimited commands in configuration files are executed during config parsing, allowing arbitrary command injection.
expected_security_impact: The malicious configuration containing a backtick-wrapped command should execute during config parsing, creating a proof file that demonstrates arbitrary command execution.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built the Docker image, executed the config parser with a malicious configuration containing backtick commands, and created the proof file with the expected marker, confirming that the command was executed during configuration parsing.
verification_method: The verify script creates a configuration file with a backtick command that writes a unique marker to a file. After config parsing, it checks for the existence and content of the proof file. The presence of the marker confirms command execution occurred during the vulnerable config.get_value() call.
evidence_produced_by_run: Configuration file with backtick command injection was successfully created
The config.get_value() method processed the backtick-delimited command
The proof file was created at /tmp/evohunt_poc_proof.txt
The unique marker was found in the proof file, confirming command execution
This demonstrates that any user with write access to glances.conf can execute arbitrary commands

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh mounts the target repository from /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_4c338aa8/repo as read-only into the container at /target-repo.
